sources.auditNotes: Official Anthropic skills repo. MIT license confirmed in repository root LICENSE file. Items are workflow and format examples, not stack-specific patterns. Haus equivalents already exist for most workflow primitives.

items.name: pdf
items.summary: Skill for extracting and analysing content from PDF files using Claude's native PDF reading capability.

items.name: sequential-thinking
items.summary: Skill that guides Claude to reason step-by-step before producing an answer, reducing premature conclusions.

items.name: test-driven-development
items.summary: Skill that enforces a red-green-refactor TDD loop before shipping any code change.

items.name: code-review
items.summary: Structured code review skill covering correctness, security, performance, and readability.

items.name: github-integration
items.summary: Workflow for interacting with GitHub issues, PRs, and repository state via gh CLI.

items.name: linear-integration
items.summary: Skill for reading and updating Linear issues and project state.
items.notes: Linear is not a supported stack in Haus. Rejected at inventory stage.

items.name: mcp-installation
items.summary: Skill for installing and configuring MCP servers in a Claude Code environment.

items.name: subagents
items.summary: Patterns for spawning and orchestrating Claude subagents for parallel or delegated work.

items.name: web-search
items.summary: Skill for performing web searches and synthesising results within a Claude Code session.

sources.auditNotes: MIT license confirmed. Source policy is rewrite-only — no verbatim copy or adaptation permitted. Items audited for idea extraction only; any reuse must be fully Haus-authored.

items.name: SHIP_IT
items.summary: End-to-end shipping workflow: plan, implement, verify, close. Enforces evidence-before-claims discipline.

items.name: code-review
items.summary: Opinionated code review skill emphasising intent and outcome over line-level comments.

items.name: context-management
items.summary: Techniques for minimising context window usage: load-on-demand, thin routers, progressive disclosure.

items.name: debug
items.summary: Systematic debugging workflow: reproduce, isolate, hypothesise, verify, fix.

items.name: test
items.summary: Testing workflow patterns: coverage discipline, regression guards, test-first habit.

sources.auditNotes: License not found in public-facing content. Status: candidate-only. Items recorded for reference and idea tracking only. No installation until license is resolved.

items.name: context-minimization
items.summary: Skill for aggressively reducing context window consumption via selective loading and summarisation.

items.name: token-budgeting
items.summary: Techniques for tracking and managing token budget across multi-step Claude Code sessions.

sources.auditNotes: Public discovery index listing community Claude Code skills. No license found. Used as a discovery signal for auditing other sources, not as a content source.

items.name: skills.sh index
items.summary: Community-maintained discovery index of Claude Code skills. Used as a search signal for auditing candidate sources.
items.notes: Index only. Individual listed items must be audited independently.

sources.auditNotes: Claude Code skills registry and discovery service. No license found. Used as a discovery signal only.

items.name: prpm registry
items.summary: Claude Code skills registry offering search and discovery. Used as an external discovery signal.
items.notes: Registry only. Individual listed items must be audited independently.

sources.auditNotes: GitHub organisation with Claude Code skill examples. No explicit license found across reviewed repositories. Ideas around skill organisation and co-location patterns were noted.

items.name: skill-organization
items.summary: Skill organisation patterns: co-located references directories, flat naming conventions, explicit use-when sections.
items.notes: Unknown license. Structural ideas only; no artifact copy.

sources.auditNotes: Skill packaging and validation framework for Claude Code. No license found. Ideas around machine-readable schemas and CI validation gates were noted.

items.name: packaging
items.summary: Skill packaging conventions: directory structure, metadata schema, and distribution format.
items.notes: Unknown license. Schema ideas informed Haus source-decisions schema design.

items.name: validation
items.summary: Skill validation tooling: lint rules, schema checks, and CI gate patterns.
items.notes: Unknown license. Validation gate patterns informed yarn sources:decisions CI gate.

sources.auditNotes: Python-first multi-agent orchestration framework. No primitives are usable in a Haus context — Python is an unsupported stack. All items rejected at inventory stage.

items.name: python-sdk
items.summary: Python SDK for building multi-agent workflows with VoltAgent orchestration.
items.notes: Python is an unsupported stack in Haus. Rejected at inventory stage.

items.name: workflow-engine
items.summary: Python-based workflow orchestration engine for agent pipelines.
items.notes: Python is an unsupported stack in Haus. Rejected at inventory stage.

sources.auditNotes: Community-curated collection index for Claude Code skills and tooling. Used as a discovery signal only. No individual items extracted for installation.

items.name: everything.cc index
items.summary: Community-curated collection index of Claude Code skills, agents, hooks, and tooling. Used as a discovery signal.
items.notes: Index only. Items must be audited individually before any can be installed.
